q: The air flow moving above the top curved surface of the wing has a ___ distance to travel.
Air above the wing needs to go ___ to have the same transit time as air along the lower surface.
The velocity of air flow is high along the ___ surface of the wing.
The velocity of air flow is low along the ___ surface of the wing.
High velocity of air above the wing causes a ___ pressure.
Low velocity of air below the wing causes a ___ pressure.
The difference in pressure above and below the wing provides a ___ on the wing.
When the piston of the sprayer is pressed, the fast moving air causes a ___ pressure at the tip of the nozzle.
The liquid inside the sprayer container is at a ___ pressure.
The higher pressure inside the sprayer causes the liquid to rush up through the ___ and out the nozzle.
The Magnus effect is a particular manifestation of ___ theorem.
The Magnus effect is commonly associated with a ___ object moving through a fluid.
A spinning ball drags some of the ___ around with it.
The dragging of air by a spinning ball causes a difference in pressure of the air on opposite sides, producing the ___ of the ball.
The Magnus effect is named after the German physicist Heinrich Gustav ___.
Heinrich Gustav Magnus described the effect in ___.
During cyclones, moving air gains speed above the roof of a house, causing ___ pressure on the roof.
The pressure inside the house is ___ than the pressure above the roof during a cyclone.
The greater pressure inside the house during a cyclone can ___ the roof.
